questions: Locate the digital resource developed as a joint project between the IUSSP and UNFPA to provide updated methods for demographic estimation from limited or defective data, serving as a direct lineage to UN Manual X. Within this resource, identify the specific chapter or section authored by T.A. Moultrie that addresses the assessment and adjustment of birth statistics. Provide the full suggested citation for this section, explicitly listing the publication year, the chapter title, all editors of the volume, and the publisher.

rounds.answer_a:
The joint IUSSP–UNFPA project is the online compendium “Tools for Demographic Estimation.” Within this resource, the section written by T. A. Moultrie that deals specifically with evaluating and correcting civil-registration data is titled “Assessment and Adjustment of Birth Statistics.”  
Suggested citation (verbatim from the site):  
Moultrie, T. A. (2013). Assessment and adjustment of birth statistics. In: Moultrie, T. A., Dorrington, R. E., Hill, A. G., Hill, K., Timæus, I. M., & Zaba, B. (eds). Tools for Demographic Estimation. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population. [1]

This chapter offers practitioners a step-by-step framework for diagnosing inconsistencies in reported live-birth data, estimating levels of under-registration, and producing adjusted series suitable for fertility analysis when vital-statistics systems are incomplete or defective. It builds upon the demographic measurement principles first codified in United Nations Manual X, updating them with modern analytical techniques and spreadsheet tools that can be applied in data-poor settings. The inclusion of both conceptual guidance and worked examples makes it an essential reference for national statistical offices and researchers aiming to correct biased birth counts and derive reliable fertility indicators from imperfect administrative sources.

## References
[1] Tools for Demographic Estimation. “Assessment and adjustment of birth statistics” by T. A. Moultrie, 2013. International Union for the Scientific Study of Population. https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/content/assessment-and-adjustment-birth-statistics
rounds.answer_b:
The digital resource you're referring to is "Tools for Demographic Estimation," developed jointly by the International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP) and the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA). This online manual serves as the modern successor to UN Manual X, providing updated methodological guidance for demographic analysis in contexts with limited or defective data.

T.A. Moultrie authored Chapter XVIII titled "The Relational Gompertz Model" within this resource. This chapter specifically addresses methods for assessing and adjusting birth statistics through the application of the relational Gompertz model, which is particularly useful for evaluating the completeness of birth registration and vital statistics systems in developing countries [1][2].

The full suggested citation for this section is:

Moultrie, T.A. (2013). "The Relational Gompertz Model." In Moultrie, T.A., Dorrington, R.E., Hill, A.G., Hill, K., Timæus, I.M., and Zaba, B. (eds.), Tools for Demographic Estimation. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population [1][2].

This comprehensive resource was published in 2013 and made freely available online at demographicestimation.iussp.org. The manual represents a collaborative effort among leading demographers to provide practical guidance for demographic estimation techniques, maintaining continuity with the methodological framework established by UN Manual X while incorporating advances in demographic methods developed over recent decades [1][2].

## References

[1] Moultrie, T.A., Dorrington, R.E., Hill, A.G., Hill, K., Timæus, I.M., and Zaba, B. (eds.) (2013). Tools for Demographic Estimation. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population.

[2] Moultrie, T.A. (2013). "The Relational Gompertz Model." In Tools for Demographic Estimation. Paris: IUSSP. Available at: demographicestimation.iussp.org
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the specific section title ("Assessment and adjustment of birth statistics") that matches the query's phrasing almost verbatim. Agent B identified a different chapter ("The Relational Gompertz Model") which is a specific method within the resource but not the section described by the user. Agent A also provided a perfect citation and context. Note: Both agents cited 2013 (the website copyright) rather than the 2011 print/PDF date listed in the Ground Truth, which is acceptable for a digital resource query.
